mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: focal segmental glomerulosclerosis, nephrosclerosis, microscopic hematuria, decreased glomerular filtration rate
hyperlipidemia (hypercholesterolemia), spleenomegaly (not mentioned but often associated)
elevated erythrocyte sedimentation rate, abnormal blood sodium concentration, fever (implied by "elevated body temperature" which is not listed)
rash (erythema), joint involvement (not explicitly stated but implied by polydipsia and polyuria - Polakisuria), kidney damage (nephritis suggested by proteinuria and renal dysfunction)
skin findings (hyperpigmented macules or patches, angiofibromas, shagreen patch, periungual fibroma), seizures (not explicitely stated but can be part of the syndrome)

hypohydrosis bulbosa (can cause dryness leading to hyperhidrosis), cornea verticillata (not mentioned but ocular symptoms are common), hypertension, cardiac involvement (not specified but may lead to heart failure)
proteinuria, increased erythrocyte sedimentation rate, hypocomplementemia (C3 level usually reduced in cryoglobulinemic patients)
nephrotic syndrome (proteinuria, edema, hypoalbuminemia), raised acute phase reactants (CRP, ESR)

mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.disease_name: Neurodevelopmental disorder with epilepsy and hemochromatosis
mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: hyperpigmentation, arthralgias, liver cirrhosis, hypouricemia (opposite of hyperuricemia)
hypertension, sweating (polydipsia implies excessive thirst due to dehydration caused by diaphoresis)
